docket_entries.description: ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE AND DISMISS UNVERIFIED COMPLAINT
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-27-2012 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER/NOTICE OF RULING FILED DENYING DEFT'S MTN TO QUASH SERVICE AND DISMISS COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEGALMATCH.COM

EXEMPT COLLECTIONS (RULE 3.740), COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEGALMATCH.COM AS TO DEFENDANT SAHL, BRAD S. SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  FEB-21-2012

NOTICE AND MOTION TO QUASH SERVCIE AND MOTION TO DISMISS UNVERIFIED CIVIL COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION, AFFIDAVIT, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES FILED BY DEFENDANT SAHL, BRAD S. HEARING SET FOR FEB-27-2012 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302
OPPOSITION TO DEFT'S MTN TO QUASH SERVICE AND DISMISS COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION FILED BY PLAINTIFF LEGALMATCH.COM

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRAD SAHL'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVCIE AND MOTION TO DISMISS UNVERIFIED CIVIL COMPLAINT FOR LACK OF PERSONAL JURISDICTION IS DENIED. DEFENDANT CONSENTED TO CALIFORNIA JURISDICTION BY ENTERING INTO AN AGREEMENT PROVIDING THAT ANY DISPUTES ABOUT THE AGREEMENT WILL BE RESOLVED IN A CALIFORNIA COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED.
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

calendar.matters:
Notice And Motion To Quash Servcie And Motion To Dismiss Unverified Civil Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction, Affidavit
Off Calendar Feb-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ken A. LaMance, Esq., of the Law Offices of Ken A. LaMance, Tel. 415.946.0856, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

No other appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT BRAD SAHL'S Motion To Quash Servcie And Motion To Dismiss Unverified Civil Complaint For Lack Of Personal Jurisdiction is Denied. Defendant consented to California jurisdiction by entering into an agreement providing that any disputes about the agreement will be resolved in a California court. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, not reported.
